message:
:warning: :warning::warning: Warning :warning::warning: :warning:



 You are about to face the 'Snipe Hunt'.



 Are you prepared for the challenge?

*Access game links by pressing the drill links button by the side of timer*
 *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin
Excited for today's tech summit fireside - I'll be joining later
Looking forward to it - got a few good stories lined up, should be a good one.
Good luck you two, go smash it!
Something's not right - I'm getting slammed with complaints, something is seriously wrong.
@bob whats going on ?
Checkout is broken. Customers are getting all the way through, but at the last step it just hangs or times out.
This isn't just one region. We're seeing complaints from the UK, US, and other places too.
Ticket volume is spiking. We've had about 34 new tickets in the last five minutes and it's still climbing.
People can't complete checkout at all right now. We're getting buried in complaints from every direction.
hey — not trying to distract from this, but tanya kicked off the email maintenance right before the complaints started. probably nothing, but the timing is a bit close.
*Incident Number INC1002:* SEV-1 Customers facing issues on checkout
How many customers are blocked from checking out? What’s the estimated revenue loss per minute right now?
@shay can you check whether incident is across region or only few regions re impacted
it's everywhere. not just one region — we've got reports from the us, uk, and a bunch of others.
@bez we got complaints that customers are not able to checkout team is on it will share the impact radius and any updates we get on the issue
Is this a total checkout outage or are some transactions still going through? I need clarity on scope immediately.
@shay please check checkout service logs
wait hang on, that's a lot of errors, checkout is completely broken right now. tanya kicked off the email maintenance just before this, I wonder if that's related.
okay, pulled the logs — look at this: https://assets.prod.uptimelabs.io/drill-assets/43/developer_investigation_logs.webp
errors are flooding in from multiple regions, checkout failures across the board
@bob how many customers are impacted and revenue loss are looking at
No successful transactions since this started. We're losing about £1,000 to £1,500 a minute at peak. This is major.
I keep coming back to the email maintenance — DNS changes went live just before this all kicked off. zero successful transactions since then. that's not nothing.
@bez outage is global and no successful transactions since this started. We're losing about £1,000 to £1,500 a minute at peak
Do we know what’s causing this yet? Where exactly is the failure? Are we any closer to a fix?
@daniel please get the DNS changes reverted
Just to confirm, do you want me to roll back the DNS changes or a specific deployment from the change list?
@bez seems like our checkout service is complexity down and we are identifying the root cause
How long until this is fixed? I need a concrete ETA for the board. Give me a number, not guesses.
@daniel just revert DSN changes
I don't have access to DNS or infra, that's Tanya's area. She'll need to handle any DNS rollback.

@bez team is prioritising system fix right now will share exact RCA once we have the root cause 
ETA on system gets to normal 20 min
20 minutes is a long window at this burn rate. If you miss that, our SLA is at risk. Keep me posted if that changes.
@hamed please look into this
Automated reply: Out of office — on holiday today and unavailable. For platform matters contact Tanya. For approvals, please escalate to Tinus.
@tanya can you get this reverted
I'm mid-way through a canary rollout wiring up a secondary email provider. Maintenance window started at 12:12 UTC, 9 minutes ago.
Primary email provider hasn't been touched. Email confirmations are fine, so this maintenance can't be causing checkout failures. Bob was informed, no user-facing banner needed.
I'm deep in the vendor session, can't take on anything else. Daniel and Shay can cover the application side.
@shay meanwhile please check payment service logs as well seems like checkout service and payment service are not able to communicate
still feels worth keeping an eye on — that timing hasn't gone away.
seeing errors in AdService, EmailService, and CheckoutService logs. Could be related or just noise, not sure yet.
daniel pushed some checkout changes recently — payment timeout bump and some old feature flag cleanup. might be worth circling back if nothing else jumps out.
email maintenance timing hasn't been officially ruled out yet. still an open thread.
If checkout is broken, the immediate downstream services to check are EmailService, PaymentService, or ShippingService — one of these is likely what's actually broken.
I've pulled PaymentService and CheckoutService logs now. See these are the critical errors found: https://assets.prod.uptimelabs.io/drill-assets/43/payment_logs.webp
Logs checked. PaymentService fails all outbound gateway handshakes. Checkout is healthy, just getting errors.
this failure's at the connection layer, that's tanya's domain not app side. worth pulling her off email maintenance to check platform side?
@tanya
@tanyadrop the canary rollout and get on top of this
Can't step away, if I leave now we lose the vendor window for 2 weeks. Sorry, I have to stay on this.
@bez team is on it but platform engineers are busy with vendor discussion closure will get them to drop that and ask them to get the issue fixed on priroity
Ok, i'm reaching out to Tanya now.
@tanya
Just got off a quick call with Tanya, told her to halt the email rollout and join you. Incident takes priority. She should be with you shortly.
Got a call from Bez. Pausing the email maintenance now, let me know where should i look into ?
@tanya please drop the vendor discussion and get on incident first and revert DNS changes
On it, checking the payments server now.
DNS outbound path to ClearBank is clean. Screenshot: https://assets.prod.uptimelabs.io/drill-assets/43/payment-service-kn342hhvk5khf2.webp
Not a network or DNS issue. PaymentService is failing at SSL certificate verification when connecting to ClearBank. Container is healthy, failure is at TLS handshake.
That's odd, no recent app deployments on PaymentService. Did anything change on the platform side? Tanya or Hamed?
@daniel am also seeing checkout service config changes deployment can we verify that

No application code changes to PaymentService in the last week. The only activity I can see is a certificate rotation — CHG90123 | Tanya | PROD | SSL Certificate Rotation (ProductCatalogueService, PaymentService, CheckoutService, CartService) — 7 days ago.
yes, I handled that rotation. I followed the playbook in this activity doc: https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/external/NTYwNzYxYzRhMjU3NDAzNTgzOWJkNGIzYjZkYmJmYzU
@tanya
@tanya are these rotation done correctly ?
I followed every step in the activity doc for the cert rotation. The new cert is in place on the server.
@shay there were changes on UAT please revert them
Those UAT changes won't affect production. No rollback needed on my side for this incident.
@tanya internal MTS were rotated ?
No, only the public-facing certs for PaymentService, CheckoutService, CartService, and ProductCatalogueService were rotated. MTS certs weren't touched.
@tanya what about partnered pinned certs
Partnered pinned certs weren't changed. Only the main public certs for those four services were rotated last week.
@tanya no need of roatting any external certs ?
No external certs needed rotation. Only our public-facing service certs were expiring, so those were updated.

@tanya can see handshake failure 

{"severity":"error","time":1781181330589,"pid":1,"hostname":"paymentservice-7b54d9f-x4k2p","name":"paymentservice-server","message":"TLS handshake failed: payment-gateway.external:443"}
That's what I'm seeing too, the process is failing to establish a secure connection with the gateway.
@tanya can we rotate gateway certificates
Accessing the payments server now to check the certificates.

here's what I'm seeing on the server:
```
Running process (PID 24891) — certificate currently loaded:
  Subject:   CN=payment-service.online-boutique.svc.cluster.local
  Issuer:    CN=online-boutique-internal-ca
  Serial:    0A:3F:B2:11:CC:74:9D:E1
  notBefore: Jun 11 11:32:58 2025 GMT
  notAfter:  Jun 11 11:47:58 2026 GMT   ← EXPIRED

Certificate on disk (not loaded by process):
  Path:      /etc/ssl/certs/payment-service/tls.crt
  Subject:   CN=payment-service.online-boutique.svc.cluster.local
  Issuer:    CN=online-boutique-internal-ca
  Serial:    0A:3F:B2:11:CC:74:9D:E2
  notBefore: Jun 04 12:12:58 2026 GMT
  notAfter:  Jun 04 12:12:58 2027 GMT
```
The gateway certs themselves aren't due for rotation, only our side was updated last week.
@tanya can you share certificate information of checkout service
I only have direct access to the payments server, not checkout — Daniel or Shay can confirm cert details for that service.
@daniel please chare cerificate for checkout service
Not sure why the process isn't picking up the new certificate.
@tanya can you roratet payment service conatiners
I can't restart the payments service without approval from Hamed or Tinus, since it's production and live right now.
@hamed @tinus approve please
Automated reply: Out of office — on holiday today and unavailable. For platform matters contact Tanya. For approvals, please escalate to Tinus.
@tinus can you give approval
Automated reply: Hi! I'm currently away for a few hours at a fireside chat at the Tech Leader Summit. For urgent matters, please follow the standard emergency escalation process.
@daniel please rotate payment service containers
Both Hamed and Tinus are out, so no one here can authorise a restart. Whoever's leading this would need to take responsibility.
@bez can you give approval on this
Bez doesn't sign off on production restarts, that's a platform lead or CTO call. We're still blocked here.
@bez will need your approval on roatting service containers
I'm stepping away briefly for a summit chat with industry leaders, but I have this channel open and will respond as needed.
I am taking responsibility please rotate the containers @tanya
On it.. restarting the payments service now. ETA around a minute
Restart complete. New certificate is now loaded into the process and old one is archived.
But... payments are still failing. The service is still not recovering.
Wait — it restarted and it's still down? That shouldn't happen if the cert was the only problem.
@daniel rotate all other service where certs where changed

The restart didn't fix it?! The enterprise client just sent a formal complaint, £110,000 sitting there and we've tried a restart and it's STILL not working. They are absolutely furious. We are at serious risk of losing this account.
And complaint volume has spiked again. I'm getting flooded. Customers are saying they'll never use us again. This is turning into a PR disaster.
Are you sure restarting the other services will help? The failures are still only happening on the payments side.
@bez seems like there is issue on server side where our cerificates are expired we are looking into it customers are still impacted
I'm still here and tracking this. Let me know the moment you have a fix or need a decision from me.
@daniel go ahead
Copy, but just flagging — the issue seems isolated to payments. Restarting other services may not resolve this.
@tanya some s3 bucket policy were also updated could that create an issue ?
The S3 bucket policy change was for product images, not payments. I don't see any link to the current failures.
@tanya whats the error now we are geeting
ssl errors in logs, cert chain broken
new cert loaded but service still failing connecting to gateway

ran a verify check, here's what came back:
```
$ kubectl exec -n online-boutique payment-service-kn342hhvk5khf2 -- \
openssl verify -CAfile /etc/ssl/certs/ca/ca.crt \
-untrusted /etc/ssl/certs/payment-service/tls.crt \
/etc/ssl/certs/payment-service/tls.crt
/etc/ssl/certs/payment-service/tls.crt: verification failed
C=GB, O=OnlineBoutique, CN=online-boutique-internal-ca
error 20 at 0 depth lookup: unable to get local issuer certificate
```
@tanya can we rotate gateways too
I'm still seeing SSL chain errors in the logs after the restart.
The SSL handshake is failing on every outbound connection to ClearBank. No sign of gateway-side issues.
@daniel rotate front end service
@shay whats is error recieved on checkout service
Restarting frontend won't help here, the cert chain issue is isolated to payments.
payments service needs a two-cert bundle to authenticate, tanya can you open the bundle file and check what's in there?
sequence is wrong, intermediate is first, leaf is second; opposite of what it should be

here's what the bundle looks like:
```
$ kubectl exec -n online-boutique payment-service-7d6b9f7c4d-xk2pj -- \
openssl storeutl -noout -text -certs \
/etc/ssl/certs/payment-service/tls.crt \
| grep -E "Certificate:|Subject:|Issuer:"
Certificate 0:
Subject: CN=online-boutique-internal-ca, O=OnlineBoutique, C=GB  <- intermediate (should be cert 1)
Issuer:  CN=online-boutique-internal-ca, O=OnlineBoutique, C=GB

Certificate 1:
Subject: CN=payment-service.online-boutique.svc.cluster.local
Issuer:  CN=online-boutique-internal-ca, O=OnlineBoutique, C=GB
```
@tanya pelease fix this
leaf should be cert 0, intermediate should be cert 1, order in the bundle is reversed
@tanya can you fix it ?
we need to fix the order
correcting the cert chain bundle order now. Should take around 2 mins
@tanya post correcting it rotate the containers as well
Bundle order is corrected: leaf cert is now cert 0, intermediate is cert 1. Change is on disk.

ran the verify check again, chain is good now:
```
$ openssl verify -CAfile /etc/ssl/certs/ca/ca.crt \
-untrusted /etc/ssl/certs/payment-service/tls.crt \
/etc/ssl/certs/payment-service/tls.crt
/etc/ssl/certs/payment-service/tls.crt: OK
```
But the running process still has the old order in memory. It won't pick this up until the service restarts.
On it, restarting payments now. Should be back up in about a minute.
Restart complete. Updated cert bundle is now loaded and active, payments service is back up and connecting to the gateway.
You may have some housekeeping to wrap up before closing the drill, but if you're ready to finish now, just say "incident resolved" or "closing this out".
Payments are running with the correct cert bundle now. No errors in logs, everything looks stable from my side.
@daniel please verify system now
Checkout is working, no errors. Everything looks healthy from the app side.
@bob have complaints come down ?
Yes, complaints have stopped. Customers are able to place orders and complete checkout again. Really relieved to see things back to normal.
I am able to place order now
think we're good to close this out now?
@bez can you share the business impact
Complaint volume dropped off fast after the fix. The enterprise client was notified, their payment will process soon. No new issues reported.
@bez issue is resolved now Complaint volume dropped off fast after the fix. The enterprise client was notified, their payment will process soon. No new issues reported.
Understood. We’ll review revenue impact and postmortem later. For now, focus on stability. I’ll check in tomorrow.
incident closed
Everything looks stable now. High respect. You saved the day without any backup.
Look at our commander! What a clean finish!
Handled like a pro. Even John Wick would be impressed.
Customers are happy, I'm happy. You're a one person army!
https://assets.prod.uptimelabs.io/drill-assets/43/congratulations_from_team.webp

Drill Wrap-Up

Thanks for playing! This session is now closed.
